why_this_recipient: Rudman is among the most recognized plaintiff-side securities fraud and gatekeeper liability litigators in the U.S. RGRD has prosecuted auditor and underwriter liability claims at scale, with recoveries exceeding $200M in individual matters.
why_now: Matter involves an offshore fraud judgment where gatekeeper liability is the primary recovery path — directly in RGRD's core practice area.
practice_match: Securities fraud, auditor and underwriter liability, gatekeeper recovery — confirmed high match.
economic_match: Contingency model aligns with RGRD's operating model. Recovery target against solvent non-debtor gatekeepers is within their typical matter economics.
conflict_screen_note: No known conflict signals. RGRD is plaintiff-only firm.

v2_changes: Removed 'professional failure enabled the fraud' → 'role may warrant scrutiny'
Added 'early negotiated resolution preferred where feasible'

outreach_draft.subject: Post-Judgment Gatekeeper Recovery Assessment — Unsatisfied Fraud Judgment — Seeking Counsel
outreach_draft.body:
Dear Mr. Rudman,

I am writing to you directly regarding a post-judgment recovery matter that may align with Robbins Geller's gatekeeper liability practice.

Our client holds an unsatisfied fraud judgment against an offshore foreign corporation. Direct enforcement against the debtor is not viable. The recovery opportunity we are assessing involves potential claims or leverage against non-debtor gatekeepers — auditors and related professional advisors whose role in the transaction or related conduct may warrant scrutiny.

The immediate objective is to assess whether viable recovery may be pursued against non-debtor gatekeepers, with early negotiated resolution preferred where feasible. The matter is only of interest if counsel sees a realistic path to material recovery against solvent non-debtor actors.

Robbins Geller's track record in auditor and underwriter accountability — including the $200M Motorola recovery and your firm's sustained record in gatekeeper-related class recoveries — reflects directly relevant experience with this recovery structure.

I would welcome a brief preliminary conversation to determine whether there is sufficient alignment to discuss further. No commitment implied at this stage.

Kind regards,

Grant de Graf
CorporateXfactor
Special Situations / Post-Judgment Recovery

Website: corporatexfactor.com
Tel 212-444-1813
